parentQuestion: Which element is necessary for proper conduction of nerve impulses?

scaffolds.q: What does Na represent on the periodic table?
scaffolds.explain: Na is sodium, from its Latin name 'natrium'. Chemistry loves its Latin roots — Pursue this logic:, there's pleasure in etymology. Sodium is an alkali metal — Group 1 on the periodic table — which means it DESPERATELY wants to donate its single valence electron and become Na⁺. In the body, it exists almost exclusively as this positively charged ion. Make room for this understanding:the elegance of this chemical desperation.

scaffolds.q: A nerve impulse involves the movement of charged particles. What are charged atoms or molecules called?
scaffolds.explain: Ions are atoms or molecules with an electrical charge due to gaining or losing electrons. Sodium loses one electron to become Na⁺ (positive charge). Now observe what happens:: ions can move across membranes, and that movement — from high concentration to low — generates electrical signals. Chemistry becomes electricity. Stunning, yes? Welcome this realization:the wonder of it.
